name: NeuroGraph Cognitive Substrate
description: Spiking neural network substrate for persistent cognitive memory. STDP-based causal learning, semantic recall, spreading activation, predictive coding, and cross-session continuity.
author: E-T Systems

tools.name: neurograph_recall
tools.description: Semantic similarity search over NeuroGraph's knowledge substrate. Returns content the network has ingested that is semantically similar to the query.

tools.name: neurograph_associate
tools.description: Associative recall through the SNN's learned synaptic structure. Unlike recall (cosine search), this routes through causal connections, pattern completion, and prediction chains. The difference between searching a database and remembering.

tools.name: neurograph_stats
tools.description: Return current substrate telemetry: node/synapse/hyperedge counts, firing rate, prediction accuracy, CES status, peer bridge status.

tools.name: neurograph_ingest_file
tools.description: Ingest a file from disk into the cognitive substrate. Supports text, code, markdown, PDF, JSON, CSV, media files.

source_type.description: Override auto-detection (TEXT, CODE, MARKDOWN, PDF, etc.)

services.description: Python child process hosting the NeuroGraph SNN engine. Communicates with the TypeScript plugin via JSON-RPC over stdio.

workspace_dir.description: Working directory for checkpoints and data

ces_enabled.description: Enable Cognitive Enhancement Suite (stream parser, surfacing monitor, activation persistence)

three_factor_enabled.description: Enable three-factor learning (STDP + eligibility traces + neuromodulatory reward)

surprise_reward_scaling.description: Scaling factor for surprise-driven reward injection

rpc_methods.compact: graph.step() consolidation pass (does not own message compaction)

python.note: The 'python' section is non-standard — it documents the RPC mapping for the TypeScript bridge implementor. OpenClaw does not read this section.
